gaps: No test covers progress bar behavior during language update (stuck at 99% for extended period)
No test validates language update completion timing/performance expectations
No test covers language update specifically on Voyager 5200 or Voyager 8200 devices — all existing language update tests target Voyager Free 60+ (multi-component TWS earbuds with charge case)
No test validates progress bar stuck states or timeout behavior during DFU
No test covers the user experience of a language update that appears to hang before completing

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 5200 — Language Update Progress Bar Stuck at 99% — Completion Validation
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Headset charged >50%.
Navigate to: Device → Settings → Language Update
Initiate language update via 'Update Language' button.
Observe progress bar from 0% to 99%.
Record timestamp when progress bar first reaches 99%.
Continue observing until update completes (progress bar reaches 100% and 'Update Complete' appears).
Record timestamp when update completes.
Calculate delta between reaching 99% and completion.
generated_testcases.expected: Progress bar reaches 99% and may stall for up to 8 minutes before completing. Delta must not exceed 10 minutes. If delta > 10 minutes, this is a regression (Bug Signature: Language update stuck at 99% indefinitely). After completion, headset reboots automatically and reconnects. Language pack version in app reflects the updated language.

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 8200 UC — Language Update Progress Bar Stuck at 99% — Completion Validation
steps: Precondition: Voyager 8200 UC connected to Poly Studio Mobile app. Headset charged >50%.
Navigate to: Device → Settings → Language Update
Initiate language update.
Monitor progress bar continuously.
When progress bar reaches 99%, start a 10-minute timer.
Verify headset remains responsive (no audio dropouts, no unexpected disconnects) during the 99% stall period.
If update completes before 10 minutes, record actual stall duration.
If update does not complete within 10 minutes, log as FAIL.
generated_testcases.expected: Progress bar reaches 99% and may stall for up to 8 minutes. Headset remains connected and responsive during stall. Update completes within 10 minutes of reaching 99%. After completion, headset reboots and reconnects automatically. Actual (If Bug): Update never completes — progress bar stuck at 99% indefinitely, requiring force-kill of app or power cycle of headset.

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 5200 — Language Update Stuck at 99% — App Force-Kill During Stall
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected. Language update initiated.
Wait for progress bar to reach and stall at 99%.
Immediately force-kill the Poly Studio Mobile app (swipe from recents).
Wait 30 seconds.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe connection state and language version.
generated_testcases.expected: After reopening app, headset reconnects automatically. Language version in device settings reflects the updated language (update completed in background despite app kill). If language version is NOT updated, this is a regression (Bug Signature: Force-killing app during 99% stall causes update to abort silently, leaving headset in indeterminate state).

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 8200 UC — Language Update Stuck at 99% — Bluetooth Disconnect During Stall
steps: Precondition: Voyager 8200 UC connected. Language update initiated.
Wait for progress bar to reach and stall at 99%.
From phone Bluetooth settings, tap 'Forget' / unpair the headset.
Wait 60 seconds.
Re-pair and reconnect Voyager 8200 UC to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Settings → Language Version
generated_testcases.expected: Headset re-pairs successfully. Language version shows the updated language. If language version shows the OLD language, this is a regression (Bug Signature: Bluetooth unpair during 99% stall corrupts the language update, causing rollback to previous language).

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 5200 — Language Update — End-to-End Timing Performance Baseline
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected. Factory default language (English) active.
Navigate to: Device → Settings → Language Update
Select a different language (e.g., French).
Start a stopwatch at the moment 'Update' is confirmed.
Record progress at each 10% interval (10%, 20%, 30%... 100%).
Record the timestamp when progress bar reaches 100% and 'Update Complete' appears.
Calculate total update duration.
Repeat test 3 times with different target languages (e.g., German, Spanish, Japanese).
generated_testcases.expected: Total update duration is consistent across runs (within ±15% variance). Progress bar moves smoothly through 0-98% without stalling longer than 30 seconds at any single percentage. 99% stall may last up to 8 minutes but total update must complete within 12 minutes. If any run exceeds 15 minutes total, flag as performance regression.

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 8200 UC — Language Update — Progress Bar Never Reaches 100% — Timeout Behavior
steps: Precondition: Voyager 8200 UC connected.
Navigate to: Device → Settings → Language Update
Initiate language update.
Allow update to proceed to 99% stall.
After 10 minutes at 99%, observe app UI behavior.
Check for any timeout dialog, error message, or automatic retry.
If no UI feedback after 10 minutes, check headset functionality: make a test call, play media.
generated_testcases.expected: App should display a timeout or error message after 10 minutes of no progress (e.g., 'Update failed. Please try again.'). Headset remains functional for calls and media playback despite failed update. If app shows no error and remains stuck indefinitely with no user feedback, this is a regression (Bug Signature: No timeout mechanism — user has no indication that update has failed and no way to recover without force-killing app).

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 5200 — Language Update — Rapid Initiation During 99% Stall (Concurrent Request)
steps: Precondition: Voyager 5200 connected. Language update in progress, stalled at 99%.
While stalled at 99%, tap 'Update Language' button again.
Observe app response.
Check if a second update is queued, rejected, or causes undefined behavior.
Wait for original update to complete (or timeout).
Verify final language version.
generated_testcases.expected: App should disable the 'Update Language' button during an active update, or show a toast: 'Update already in progress'. Initiating a second update must not corrupt the first update. Final language version must match the first requested language. If second update overwrites or cancels the first, this is a regression (Bug Signature: Concurrent language update requests cause device to enter undefined language state).

generated_testcases.title: Voyager 8200 UC — Language Update — Low Battery Interruption During 99% Stall
steps: Precondition: Voyager 8200 UC connected. Headset battery at ~15-20%.
Initiate language update.
Allow update to reach 99% stall.
Allow battery to drain naturally or via continuous audio playback to accelerate drain.
When headset announces 'Battery low' / 'Power off', observe app behavior.
After headset powers off, charge headset to >50%.
Power on and reconnect. Check language version.
generated_testcases.expected: If headset powers off during 99% stall, the language update should resume or roll back gracefully. After recharge and reconnect, language version should either show the updated language (if flash completed before power loss) or the original language (if flash was interrupted). Headset must not be bricked or stuck in a boot loop. If headset becomes unresponsive or fails to power on after charging, this is a critical regression (Bug Signature: Low battery power-off during language update 99% stall corrupts firmware, bricking the device).
